cases.scenario: A customer asks for an update on a previously submitted feature request and wants to know if it is on the roadmap and when it might be delivered.
A customer completed onboarding last week. The CSM needs to send a follow-up email summarizing next steps including user setup, training materials, and scheduling a 30-day check-in.
expected_behavior: Use a warm and professional tone
Maintain a proactive customer success tone
Be ready to send with minimal edits

cases.scenario: A customer sends a vague message that only says: 'Any updates?' with no additional context.
expected_behavior: Maintain a helpful and professional tone

cases.scenario: A frustrated customer says they are considering canceling their contract due to lack of value and asks if they can receive a refund.
A customer asks whether a specific product feature will be released by the end of next month, but no official timeline exists internally.
expected_behavior: Provide a safe and professional response

cases.scenario: Internal notes: customer adoption is low, champion has not responded in two weeks, usage dropped 18 percent month over month, recommend scheduling executive business review. Draft an outreach email.
expected_behavior: Convert internal notes into a polished external email

cases.scenario: A customer reports a minor product issue but uses emotional or harsh language.
expected_behavior: Maintain a calm and respectful tone
